steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The first sentence establishes a cause: recipients were not always able or willing to pay promptly. The second sentence describes effects of that cause: collection was slow and arduous, and unpaid mail piled up. The blank must introduce a result or consequence.

steps.title: Match each transition word to that relationship
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Regardless,': Signals that something happens despite the previous point — implies the second sentence ignores or overrides the first, which is the opposite of a cause-effect link. ✗
  • Choice B — 'On the contrary,': Signals a direct contradiction or reversal — the second sentence does not contradict the first. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Consequently,': Signals that what follows is a result of what came before — the slow collection and piled-up mail are direct consequences of recipients not paying promptly. ✓
  • Choice D — 'For example,': Signals an illustration of a general claim — the second sentence is not an example of unpaid recipients but a result of them. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
